meaning: n.强迫,强制,[心]强迫性冲动
examples: You are under no compulsion to pay immediately.
She seemed dead with fear—yes, she was under his compulsion.
She shunned bodily ornamentation and strove to use only the natural movements of her body, undistorted by acrobatic exaggeration and stimulated only by internal compulsion.
But former Conservative AM Jonathan Morgan, now working as a lobbyist, said there was no need for compulsion.
The EU has to be more flexible or the compulsion to integrate will lead to protectionism.
